Advisor: Got it. And any dependents we should plan around—kids at home, college support, anyone financially dependent on you?

Advisor: Hi Joseph, hi Emily—good to meet you both. I’m glad you came in. Before we get into the numbers, what prompted you to set this up now?
Client 1: Thanks. I’m Joseph. Honestly, we’ve got a bunch of accounts and I want a clear plan—like, are we actually on track or just busy?
Client 2: And I’m Emily. I just want it to feel… manageable. Retirement feels close enough that I don’t want surprises.
Advisor: That makes sense. Let’s start high-level—income, spending, debts, then we’ll map the accounts. Joseph, what’s your household gross income per year, all-in?

Advisor: Got it—Washington. And country-wise, just confirming you’re in the US?
Client 1: Yes, US.
Advisor: Perfect. Now, risk—when markets swing, how would you describe your overall comfort level? Conservative, moderate, aggressive… what fits best for you two?
Client 2: I’d say moderate. I don’t want anything too wild.
Advisor: Okay. When you say “not too wild,” do you mean you can tolerate normal ups and downs as long as there’s a plan, or do drops really keep you up at night?
